chunk_summary: This section discusses affordable and safe dietary protein sources for children, emphasizing the importance of protein quality and quantity in childhood nutrition, with references to authoritative guidelines and research.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of protein quality and quantity in childhood nutrition.
Identify affordable and safe dietary protein sources for children.
Learn about recommended dietary allowances (RDAs) for protein in children based on authoritative guidelines.
Explore the importance of protein in the growth and development of infants and children.
key_facts: Adding one katori of dal at lunch and curd at dinner is a cost-effective way to meet protein needs in children.
The ICMR-NIN (2020) provides specific RDAs for protein intake in Indian children.
High-quality protein sources are essential for optimal growth and development during childhood.
The FAO (2013) emphasizes the importance of dietary protein quality evaluation in human nutrition.
ESPGHAN (2022) provides specific guidelines for enteral nutrition in preterm infants.
reference_quote: Let us add one katori of dal at lunch and curd at dinner; this is cheaper and safer than supplements.
document_key_topics: Protein quantity and quality in childhood nutrition
Guidelines for protein intake in children
